Treasury Chief Secretary Lucy Rigby cited funding the scrapping of the two-child benefit limit when grilled over the government's 6 per cent cap on the interest rate paid on some loans. While the change means the rate is lower than it would otherwise be, critics say that it is still too high and will benefit well-off graduates more than those from poorer backgrounds. Ms Rigby defended the level set by Chancellor Rachel Reeves in April on Plan 2 loans , which came against a national clamour for action backed by Tory leader Kemi Badenoch. Appearing in front of the Treasury Committee alongside Skills Minister Baroness Smith this afternoon the Treasury Minister said: 'Politics is about choices and the Chancellor has chosen to prioritise a number of things including lifting the two-child benefit cap for example.'

  • 2 videos

I found moderate fasting radically improved my weight loss journey when I was stuck at a weight with Mounjaro. If you have never fasted before, try a one day fast where you eat only a protein rich/low carb meal late afternoon followed by a ‘normal’ day of eating, then another fast on Day 3 and so on. Take Saturday and Sunday off from this. So fast on Monday, Weds and Friday. You should find this kickstarts your weight loss especially if you eat fairly healthily on the normal 3 meals-a-day days. Good luck.

When I started taking Mounjaro last year I found losing weight was easy. In eight months I lost two stone, bringing me just a few pounds short of my goal, but then I hit the dreaded MJ plateau.

I always said having Jesy around must have been exhausting. I had a friend who would ring morning, noon and night, always crying, 'can you come, I need you' I did as I was the only person she had all to the detriment of my own relationship. Sods law, my partner and I split, she met somebody and skipped off, I saw her once afterwards and all she wanted to talk about was wallpaper. Moral of the story, I learned that you put yourself first in this life, other people's misery is not mine, and they're not my responsibility.

Perrie Edwards has revealed she has cut out 'difficult' Jesy Nelson from her life for good as she addressed Little Mix feud in a new interview on Wednesday.  The singer has accused her former bandmate of not taking accountability for her behaviour in the final months of her time in the girl group.  Jesy left Little Mix in December 2020 after nine years in the band, saying at the time the pressure had taken a toll on her mental health.

A close friend of mine does this and the level of manipulation that goes on at these events in particular, is staggering. My friend has quoted for me several times for my travel back and forth to Asia, she has never managed to get a better deal than I can get direct from hotels or airlines, but I’ve booked hotels through her out of pity really. She talks about “the business” like she’s an international business woman when in reality she is exactly the same as the lady in this article. I value our friendship so I can’t say anything to her or I’ll be labelled unsupportive and all sorts of other things she’s been trained to respond to the doubters. I’ve been looking to downsize for the last two years. I wouldn’t touch a retirement home unless I had enough capital not to be concerned about service charges. My problem is the service charges on private flats. Coastal towns in the SE already suffer from inflated prices as Londoners move out and commute. But when you check out ground rent and service charges, then take out estate agent fees and moving costs, I’m far better off staying put and hiring a bit of help with gardening now and again.

Bereaved families are inheriting 'almost unsellable' retirement flats that cost them hundreds of pounds for every month they are stuck with them. In some cases, they are slashing the asking price in half just to get a sale, leaving them with a fraction of the inheritance their loved one had intended.

23 year old son who she is raising herself! Really - says it all!!!

Once 'the undisputed belle of Scotland', Lady Georgina this week told the society bible that she has endured a decade and a half of misery - including teetering on the brink of bankruptcy and coping with the grief of her husband John's sudden death at 54. John, the late owner of McEwens of Perth - the Scottish department store dubbed the 'Harrods of the North' - and Gulf War veteran, died from a heart attack three years ago, as Georgina said she thinks of him 'every minute of every day' while raising their son, Hercules, by herself. His passing was an even greater tragedy because it came after they had stabilised their lives, after withstanding uncertainty and financial hardship when McEwens collapsed with debts of over £4million in March 2016.

The Three Lions are set to take on Costa Rica on Wednesday night in their second friendly out in the States, after beating New Zealand 1-0 on Saturday. It is set to be Thomas Tuchel's final chance to look at his side in match action before they get their campaign under way on June 17 against Croatia, but the game is now in doubt. Disclosure Day divides critics: Steven Spielberg's alien drama is branded 'the most disappointing film of the year' in savage reviews as others praise legendary director's 'spellbinding and masterful' cinematic return

Critics have been left scratching their heads after previewing Steven Spielberg's summer blockbuster Disclosure Day. Whilst the plot of the legendary director's latest alien drama has been branded baffling by most reviewers, the critics are bamboozled over how to rate the movie.  The movie follows Emily Blunt's meteorologist and Josh O'Connor's cybersecurity expert whistleblower who find themselves at the center of a movement to expose the government's cover-up of extraterrestrial secrets. Colin Firth, Colman Domingo and Eve Hewson also star.

  • My wife is Italian and we are often at weddings in Italy. Remember 10 years ago in Ravello at my wife's niece wedding sitting with my daughter looking out over the Tyrrhenian Sea at 5 in the evening glass of champagne in my hand. It's one of those moments that just stays with you if you know what I mean. Often think back to that moment. If only I could build a time machine

While seemingly just an inconspicuous purple or green sphere hidden among other, more popular produce, this cheap vegetable is chock full of benefits.
  • 53 comments
  • share The cheap vegetable dietitians say is 'secret weapon' for weight loss

Cheaper than a parking space in Sydney.

Tucked away in the Queensland outback over 800km west of Brisbane, the entire town of Cooladdi - once a bustling rail hub of 270 people - is currently for sale. With only two permanent residents who run the local roadhouse, motel, pub, post office, shop and restaurant, this unique listing allows you to buy an entire town with its own postcode.
